message:
fix: exit 0 when gemini CLI not installed in CI mode

Gemini patcher is a post-install script that patches an existing gemini
installation. In CI/non-interactive environments where gemini isn't
installed, exit 0 with a message instead of exit 1.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -281,6 +281,10 @@ find_gemini_root() {
 
 GEMINI_ROOT=""
 if ! GEMINI_ROOT="$(find_gemini_root 2>/dev/null)"; then
+    if [ "${CI:-}" = "true" ] || [ "${NONINTERACTIVE:-}" = "1" ]; then
+        printf "  ${CROSS}  ${RED}%s${RESET}\n" "Gemini CLI not installed — nothing to patch (skipping)"
+        exit 0
+    fi
     fail "Could not find @google/gemini-cli — is it installed globally?"
 fi
 ok "Found: $GEMINI_ROOT"
